pattern: Connect every pair of strings that differ in at most two positions, then the number of connected components under union-find is the number of similarity groups.
hints: Two strings belong together when they match everywhere or differ in exactly two spots that could be swapped.
Build connectivity: link each pair that is similar, even indirectly through a chain of similar strings.
Use union-find over the indices, joining any two strings whose differing positions number zero or two, then count distinct roots.

edge_inputs.description: a chain forms one group plus an isolated word
two similar anagrams collapse into one group
single string is its own group
identical duplicates are similar and merge
differ in four positions, not connected

stress.description: permutations of a base anagram over a small letter pool so many pairs are similar
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 60))
    base = ['a', 'a', 'b', 'b', 'c', 'd']
    out = []
    for _ in range(n):
        perm = base[:]
        rng.shuffle(perm)
        out.append(''.join(perm))
    return [out]
stress.note: shared anagram pool makes adjacency dense, stressing union-find merges
